title.en: A zoo of stations: STA flavors and the bands they live in
title.zh: 站点动物园：各类 STA 及其所属频段

blocks.en: Read clause 4 of 802.11 and you meet a whole menagerie of 'STA' names: HT STA, VHT STA, HE STA, S1G STA, DMG STA, EDMG STA, CDMG STA, CMMG STA, TVHT STA, WUR STA, NGV STA, LC STA, plus the IBSS STA, the PBSS with its PCP, and the TDLS STA. It looks like a dozen different devices. It is not. Almost every one of these names is either a role a station temporarily plays, or a band-tuned PHY profile bolted onto the same 802.11 MAC. Strip the labels away and there is one animal underneath: a station.
blocks.zh: 翻开 802.11 标准的第 4 章，你会遇到一整群叫“STA”的名字：HT STA、VHT STA、HE STA、S1G STA、DMG STA、EDMG STA、CDMG STA、CMMG STA、TVHT STA、WUR STA、NGV STA、LC STA，再加上 IBSS STA、带有 PCP 的 PBSS、以及 TDLS STA。看上去像是十几种不同的设备。其实不是。这些名字几乎都只是两类东西：要么是一个站点临时扮演的“角色”，要么是叠加在同一套 802.11 MAC 上、针对某个频段调校过的“PHY 配置档”。把标签揭掉，底下永远是同一只动物：一个站点。

blocks.en: First the role-based names — these are about who coordinates the network, not about radio. An IBSS STA is just a station in an ad-hoc network with no AP: stations talk directly, formed without preplanning. A PBSS is similar, but one station volunteers as the PBSS control point (PCP), providing timing via DMG Beacon/Announce frames and handing out service periods — a station that has taken the coordinator chair, and a PBSS can only be formed by DMG stations. A TDLS STA is the sneakiest: two stations stay associated to their AP but open a direct link between themselves, tunneling the setup signaling inside Data frames so the AP need not even understand TDLS.
blocks.zh: 先看基于“角色”的名字——它们关乎的是谁来协调网络，而不是无线电本身。IBSS STA 不过是处于自组织（ad-hoc）网络里、没有 AP 的站点：站点之间直接对话，无需事先规划即可组网。PBSS 与之类似，但其中一个站点自愿担任 PBSS 控制点（PCP），通过 DMG Beacon/Announce 帧提供定时，并分配服务周期——也就是一个坐上了“协调者”席位的站点；而且 PBSS 只能由 DMG 站点组建。TDLS STA 最为巧妙：两个站点仍与各自的 AP 保持关联，却在彼此之间开一条直连链路，把建链信令封装进 Data 帧里隧道传输，使得 AP 甚至无需理解 TDLS。

blocks.en:
graph TD
    MAC["One 802.11 MAC"]
    MAC --> ROLE["Roles (who coordinates)"]
    MAC --> PHY["Band/generation PHY profiles"]
    ROLE --> IBSS["IBSS STA: ad-hoc, no AP"]
    ROLE --> PBSS["PBSS: a STA is the PCP"]
    ROLE --> TDLS["TDLS STA: direct link under an AP"]
    PHY --> GEN["HT - VHT - HE (2.4/5/6 GHz ladder)"]
    PHY --> S1G["S1G: sub-1 GHz, long range, relays"]
    PHY --> MMW["DMG/EDMG/CDMG/CMMG: 60 GHz and up"]
    PHY --> NICHE["TVHT (TV whitespace), WUR, NGV (V2X), LC (light)"]
blocks.zh:
graph TD
    MAC["同一套 802.11 MAC"]
    MAC --> ROLE["角色（谁来协调）"]
    MAC --> PHY["频段/世代 PHY 配置档"]
    ROLE --> IBSS["IBSS STA：自组织，无 AP"]
    ROLE --> PBSS["PBSS：某个 STA 担任 PCP"]
    ROLE --> TDLS["TDLS STA：AP 之下的直连链路"]
    PHY --> GEN["HT - VHT - HE（2.4/5/6 GHz 阶梯）"]
    PHY --> S1G["S1G：sub-1 GHz，远距离，带中继"]
    PHY --> MMW["DMG/EDMG/CDMG/CMMG：60 GHz 及以上"]
    PHY --> NICHE["TVHT（电视空白频段）、WUR、NGV（车联网）、LC（光）"]

blocks.en: Now the generation ladder — the names most people recognize as 'Wi-Fi versions'. HT (802.11n) brought MIMO and 100+ Mb/s; VHT (802.11ac) is an HT STA plus 80/160 MHz and DL MU-MIMO, 5 GHz only; HE (802.11ax, Wi-Fi 6) adds OFDMA and works across 1-7.125 GHz, and is itself a VHT STA when in 5 GHz. Notice the pattern: each generation is defined as 'the previous one, plus more'. They are not separate device classes — a VHT STA is an HT STA, an HE STA is a VHT STA. The same MAC, with a richer PHY profile each step up the ladder.
blocks.zh: 再看“世代阶梯”——也就是大多数人认得的那些“Wi-Fi 版本”。HT（802.11n）带来了 MIMO 和 100+ Mb/s；VHT（802.11ac）就是“HT STA 再加上 80/160 MHz 和下行 MU-MIMO”，且只工作在 5 GHz；HE（802.11ax，即 Wi-Fi 6）加入了 OFDMA，并覆盖 1–7.125 GHz，在 5 GHz 时它本身又是一个 VHT STA。注意这个规律：每一代都被定义为“上一代，再加上更多”。它们并不是彼此独立的设备类别——VHT STA 就是 HT STA，HE STA 就是 VHT STA。同一套 MAC，每往上爬一阶就配上更丰富的 PHY 配置档。

blocks.en: The 'is-a' chain is the key to reading clause 4. The standard literally writes 'A VHT STA is an HT STA that...' and 'An HE STA is also a VHT STA if operating in the 5 GHz band'. So when you see a new STA name, don't ask 'what new device is this?' — ask 'which existing STA is it, plus what?'
blocks.zh: “是一个（is-a）”的继承链，是读懂第 4 章的关键。标准里就是这么写的：“VHT STA 是一种 HT STA，外加……”、“HE STA 在 5 GHz 频段工作时也是一个 VHT STA”。所以当你看到一个新的 STA 名字时，别问“这是什么新设备？”——要问“它是哪一个已有的 STA，再加上了什么？”

blocks.en: The rest are band-specialists and niche radios, each the same MAC tuned to where it must live. S1G operates below 1 GHz for long range and low power, and defines an S1G relay (a relay AP + relay STA) to push an AP's coverage further. DMG/EDMG/CDMG/CMMG are the millimeter-wave family at 60 GHz and above, all directional and beamforming-heavy — CDMG is DMG in China's 60 GHz band, CMMG climbs to the 42-48 GHz range, EDMG pushes DMG past 20 Gb/s. TVHT reuses VHT in TV whitespace. WUR is a tiny companion radio that wakes the main one at under 1 mW. NGV is the vehicular (V2X) station that runs outside the context of a BSS at 5.9 GHz. And LC literally does Wi-Fi over light (800-1000 nm), borrowing HT/VHT/HE behavior wholesale.
blocks.zh: 其余的都是“频段专才”和“小众电台”，每一个都是同一套 MAC、针对它必须栖身的环境调校而成。S1G 工作在 1 GHz 以下，追求远距离与低功耗，并定义了 S1G 中继（一个中继 AP + 一个中继 STA），把 AP 的覆盖范围进一步推远。DMG/EDMG/CDMG/CMMG 是 60 GHz 及以上的毫米波家族，全都高度依赖定向天线与波束成形——CDMG 是工作在中国 60 GHz 频段的 DMG，CMMG 攀升到 42–48 GHz 区间，EDMG 把 DMG 推过 20 Gb/s。TVHT 在电视空白频段里复用 VHT。WUR 是一颗微型伴随电台，能以低于 1 mW 的功耗唤醒主电台。NGV 是车联网（V2X）站点，在 5.9 GHz 上以“脱离 BSS 语境（OCB）”的方式运行。而 LC 干脆把 Wi-Fi 跑在光上（800–1000 nm），整套照搬 HT/VHT/HE 的行为。

blocks.en: Two of these names break the 'always inside a BSS' assumption. A STA with dot11OCBActivated true sends Data frames outside the context of a BSS (OCB) — no authentication, no association, just immediate direct frames, ideal for fast-moving vehicles. NGV is precisely that station refined for 5.9 GHz V2X.
blocks.zh: 其中有两个名字打破了“站点总是待在某个 BSS 里”的默认假设。当 dot11OCBActivated 为 true 时，站点会以“脱离 BSS 语境（OCB）”的方式发送 Data 帧——不认证、不关联，直接即时地收发帧，非常适合高速移动的车辆。NGV 正是为 5.9 GHz 车联网（V2X）而打磨的这类站点。

summary.en: The exact 'is-a' wording, and why S1G even has a relay
summary.zh: 精确的“是一个”措辞，以及 S1G 为何需要中继

blocks.en: Clause 4.3.15 defines a VHT STA as 'an HT STA that, in addition to features supported as an HT STA, supports VHT features... but operates only in the 5 GHz band'. Clause 4.3.16 then layers HE on top: an HE STA 'is also a VHT STA if operating in the 5 GHz band' and 'is also an HT STA' in 2.4 GHz — so the generations stack rather than replace. The S1G overview (4.3.14.1) lists relay as an optional MAC feature, and 4.3.14.2 spells out why: an S1G relay (a relay AP paired with a relay STA, served by a root AP) forwards frames to expand the AP's coverage area — important precisely because sub-1 GHz S1G targets long-range, low-power links where a single hop may not reach. At the millimeter-wave end, 4.3.25 notes a DMG STA's basic channel access lets it operate in an infrastructure BSS, an IBSS, or a PBSS — one radio, three network roles — and 4.3.33 defines EDMG as a DMG STA pushed to 20 Gb/s with 4.32 GHz channels. The recurring theme across all of clause 4.3 is composition: a small MAC core, plus a role, plus a band-tuned PHY.
blocks.zh: 4.3.15 节把 VHT STA 定义为“一种 HT STA，在 HT STA 所支持的特性之外，再支持 VHT 特性……但只工作在 5 GHz 频段”。4.3.16 节随后把 HE 叠加上去：HE STA“在 5 GHz 频段工作时也是一个 VHT STA”，在 2.4 GHz 时“也是一个 HT STA”——所以各世代是层层叠加，而非彼此取代。S1G 概述（4.3.14.1）把中继列为一项可选的 MAC 特性，而 4.3.14.2 讲清了缘由：S1G 中继（一个中继 AP 配一个中继 STA，由根 AP 提供服务）转发帧以扩展 AP 的覆盖范围——这一点之所以重要，正是因为 sub-1 GHz 的 S1G 面向远距离、低功耗链路，单跳未必够得着。在毫米波这端，4.3.25 指出 DMG STA 的基本信道接入使其能在基础设施 BSS、IBSS 或 PBSS 中运行——同一台电台，三种网络角色——而 4.3.33 把 EDMG 定义为被推到 20 Gb/s、带 4.32 GHz 信道的 DMG STA。贯穿整个 4.3 节的主题就是“组合”：一个小小的 MAC 内核，加上一个角色，再加上一份针对频段调校的 PHY。
